digest.top_line: SF-SABR for NSCLC/pulmonary oligomets (N=1687): 2yr LC 90-93%, G3+ AEs 2.9% — single-fraction scales.
digest.tldr: Thoracic and GU dominated today. SF-SABR's pooled multi-institutional data (N=1687) consolidates single-fraction as a viable radiotherapy approach for primary NSCLC and pulmonary oligomets. In prostate, AUA 2026 evidence synthesis questions routine ePLND when PSMA PET is negative, with morbidity data reinforcing a more selective dissection strategy.

sites.intro: PSMA PET precision is eroding the rationale for routine ePLND across risk groups.

studies.name: ePLND vs PSMA PET/CT for Prostate Cancer Staging (AUA 2026)
studies.tldr: PSMA PET/CT NPV ~96% for LNI; 47.7% of LN mets outside ePLND template, questioning routine extended dissection.
details: 📊 Yaxley et al (BJUI 2019), N=1253 consecutive staging Ga-PSMA PET/CT: 47.7% of LN metastases fell outside ePLND anatomical template boundaries
🔍 AUA 2026 session summarizing RCT evidence, systematic review data, and a PCAN 2024 perspective piece (Roberts et al); not a single new RCT
📊 Lymphedema risk from OCR (Clinckaert systematic review): 0-14% lower limb lymphedema post-RP+PLND; 19-29% after PLND + salvage pelvic node RT (2-22% also genital)
📊 Tyritzis et al J Urol 2015, N=3544: PLND associated with 6-10x increased DVT/PE risk vs no PLND

details.text: 🔍 Conclusions by risk group (AUA 2026 slide OCR)
subdetails: Intermediate risk: PLND may safely be omitted if PSMA PET negative for LNI; missed nodes likely small, also missed by ePLND
GG3: nomograms add value alongside PSMA PET
High risk: individualized decision; adjuvant/salvage pelvic RT may increase side effects if PLND already performed

details: ⚠️ No Level 1 evidence for oncological benefit of ePLND; RCTs have not shown consistent BCR improvement; earlier ARSI use makes pN status less clinically actionable
❓ Optimal integration of targeted/radioguided/sentinel LND with PSMA PET unresolved; higher nomogram cut-off thresholds for PLND omission not yet standardized

verdict.rationale: Aggregate evidence questions routine ePLND utility: template misses 47.7% of mets, no Level 1 oncological benefit, high morbidity, and PSMA PET NPV ~96%.
verdict.audience: Clinically localized prostate cancer, intermediate-to-high risk, primary staging

open_questions: What nomogram LNI probability threshold justifies PLND omission when PSMA PET is negative, and does it differ by grade group?
How should targeted/radioguided LND be integrated with PSMA PET findings when a node-positive result would change adjuvant decisions?
With earlier ARSI use reducing the clinical impact of pN staging, does ePLND retain oncological value for any subgroup?

studies.name: Single-Fraction SABR for Primary NSCLC and Pulmonary Oligometastases (Pooled 1687 pts)
studies.tldr: SF-SABR for primary NSCLC/oligomets (N=1687): 2yr LC 90-93%, median OS 40 vs 51mo, G3+ AEs 2.9%.
details: 🔍 Pooled multi-institutional (Peter Mac, Cleveland Clinic, Roswell Park): 1200 primary NSCLC, 487 pulmonary oligomets
📊 2yr local control 90-93%; median PFS 30mo (NSCLC) vs 11mo (oligomets); isolated local/locoregional failure uncommon
📊 G3+ AEs 2.9% (23/789); G2+ 15.7% (124/789); AE data primary NSCLC only, n=789 (no Roswell Park AE data)
🔗 vs CHISEL (Lancet Oncol 2019): randomised single-fraction vs multifraction for peripheral T1-2 NSCLC; this pooled analysis scales to N=1687 and extends to oligomets setting
⚠️ Single-arm pooled analysis; no randomised comparator vs multifraction SABR; eligibility and dose heterogeneity across 3 institutions not reported
❓ Optimal SF-SABR vs multifraction selection by tumor size, location, and histology undefined; no QoL or patient-reported outcomes

verdict.rationale: Single-arm pooled analysis; no randomised comparator vs multifraction SABR. Consistent with CHISEL signal but hard maturity gate applies.
verdict.audience: SABR-eligible primary NSCLC or pulmonary oligometastases, 3-institution pooled c

open_questions: Does SF-SABR efficacy hold across tumor sizes and central/peripheral locations, or is multifraction still preferred for larger or central lesions?
What is the patient-reported toxicity and QoL profile of SF-SABR vs multifraction at 12-24 months?

Conclusions
• For intermediate risk disease PLND may safely be omitted if PSMA PET is negative for LNI,
missed LN are small and may be missed in ePLND as well
• In GG3 disease nomograms can be of additional use
•
For high-risk disease, the decision is individual and should be discussed with the patient at
length including the possibility that an adjuvant/salvage pelvic RT may increase side effects
If PLND is to be performed, then it should be in extended fashion

Inge Geraerts, Steven Joniau 2*0 and Wouter Everaerts 12.-O
• 0-14% lower limb lymphedema following Radical prostatectomy
with PLND
• 0-9% after pelvic lymph node radiotherapy
• 19-29% after PLND and salvage pelvic node radiotherapy, with
2-22% also developing genital lymphedema
• The lack of a uniform definition and standardized diagnostic
criteria for lower limb and genital lymphedema hampers the
accurate estimation of their true prevalence
Lymphadenectomy may cause harm

PERSPECTIVE
OPEN
1f) Chock for updates
Clinical
Is extended pelvic lymph node dissection REALLY required for
staging of prostate cancer in the PSMA-PET era?
Matthew J. Roberts 123, John W. Yaxley 134, Johan Stranne, Inge M. van Oort and Derya Tilki C89.10
Strengths
•Confirm metastases not detected by imaging (false-negatives)
• Accurate histopathological staging (false-positives)
• Total tumour burden reduction via excision (if nodal metastases
present)
Opportunities
•Incorporation with PSMA-PET imaging for targeted (Radioguided/
Sentinel) LND
• Improved patient selection considering prevalence of metastatic
disease (higher nomogram cut-off)
Weaknesses
• Limitation of anatomical template (missed metastases outside
template)
• Limitation of histopathology (may not detect all metastatic cells)
•Associated morbidity/complications, impact on health and quality of
life
• Increased intra-operative time
Threats
• Absent Level 1 evidence for significant oncological benefit
• Increasing demand on health services to limit low-value care/
interventions
• Surgeon proficiency/training
• Earlier use of androgen receptor signalling inhibitors and other
medications (pN status less relevant)
Alternative care pathways instead of routine eLND
PSMA-PET for staging may prompt a plan for post-operative pelvic lymph node radiotherapy,
limiting morbidity such as lymphedema, especially in the absence of a prior eND.
PCAN, 2024

Summary/Future Directions
• Low incidence of clinically significant AEs
(G3 + in 2-3%
• Local Control estimates 90-93% at 2 years
• Primary NSCLC: Median PFS 30 months and
median OS 3.5 years.
• Pulmonary Oligometastases: Median PHS 11
months and OS >4 years
• Isolated Local/Locoregional failure very
uncommon
